Class ConfLoader

java.lang.Object
servlets.ConfLoader
All Implemented Interfaces:
Servlet
public class ConfLoader extends Object implements Servlet
Application servlet that handles the /upload endpoint for configuration deployment.

This is a reference servlet bundled with the computational-graph dashboard. Library consumers may use it as-is or as a pattern for building their own upload handlers.

GET: Builds a Graph from the current topics in TopicManagerSingleton and returns an HTML visualization generated by HtmlGraphWriter.

POST: Accepts a configuration file via multipart form data (filename and file fields) or, as a fallback, plain request body and query parameters. The file is saved under config_files/, loaded through GenericConfig, and the resulting graph HTML is returned. On error, a 400 Bad Request plain-text response is sent.

Thread safety: This servlet is not thread-safe for concurrent POST requests. It shares TopicManagerSingleton state and performs filesystem writes without synchronization; concurrent uploads may race.

See Also:

  • HTTPServer.addServlet(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, servlets.Servlet)

  • Constructor Details

    • ConfLoader

      public ConfLoader()

  • Method Details

    • handle

      public void handle(RequestParser.RequestInfo ri, OutputStream toClient) throws IOException
      Processes a GET or POST request to the /upload endpoint.

      For POST requests, parses multipart or plain body content, saves the configuration file, loads agents via GenericConfig, rebuilds the graph, and writes an HTML response. For GET requests, renders the current graph without uploading. Errors are reported as 400 Bad Request.

      Specified by:
      handle in interface Servlet
      Parameters:
      ri - the parsed request, including HTTP method, parameters, and body content
      toClient - the client output stream for the HTTP response
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while writing the response

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Releases servlet resources.

      Currently a no-op; no persistent resources are held between requests.

      Specified by:
      close in interface Servlet
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during cleanup
